description: centre for documentation, information and research on Nazi persecution, forced labour and the Holocaust in Nazi Germany and its occupied regions
United States' official memorial to the Holocaust in Washington, D.C.
description_wiki: The United States Holocaust Memorial Museum (USHMM) is the United States' official memorial of the Holocaust, dedicated to the documentation, study, and interpretation of the Holocaust.

aliases: The Center for Migration Studies of New York
description: educational institute and nonpartisan think tank

description_wiki: United Nations Relief and Rehabilitation Administration (UNRRA, pronounced  UN-rə) was an international relief agency founded in November 1943 on the joint initiative of the United States, United Kingdom, USSR, and the Republic of China.

International Business Machines Corporation, doing business as IBM (nicknamed Big Blue), is an American multinational technology company headquartered in Armonk, New York, and present in over 175 countries.
The Ford Motor Company (commonly known as Ford, sometimes abbreviated as FoMoCo) is an American multinational automobile manufacturer headquartered in Dearborn, Michigan, United States.

description: largest city in Pennsylvania, United States
description_wiki: Philadelphia (  FIL-ə-DEL-fee-ə), colloquially referred to as Philly, is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Pennsylvania, and the sixth-most populous city in the United States, with a Census-estimated population of 1,574,281 in July 2025.

description: Holy See's independent state, an enclave within Rome, Italy
description_wiki: Vatican City, officially the Vatican City State (Italian: Stato della Città del Vaticano; Latin: Status Civitatis Vaticanae), often shortened as the Vatican, is a landlocked sovereign city-state.

description: capital city of the United States of America
description_wiki: Washington, D.C., officially the District of Columbia and commonly known as simply Washington or D.C., is the capital city and federal district of the United States.

description: formation of the United States Army (1918/19, 1932-74 and since 1982)
calendar introduced by Julius Caesar in 45 BC
description_wiki: The Julian calendar is a solar calendar of 365 days in every year with an additional leap day every fourth year (without exception).
Buchenwald (German pronunciation: [ˈbuːxn̩valt]; 'beech forest') was a German Nazi concentration camp established on Ettersberg hill near Weimar, Germany, in July 1937.

aliases: the Union of Soviet Socialist Republics
description_wiki: The Union of Soviet Socialist Republics (USSR), also known as the Soviet Union, was a transcontinental country that spanned much of Eurasia from 1922 until its dissolution in 1991.

description: country located primarily in North America
description_wiki: The United States of America (USA), also known as the United States (U.S.) or America, is a country primarily located in North America.

description: German Nazi politician, military leader, and convicted war criminal (1893–1946)
description_wiki: Hermann Wilhelm Göring (or Goering; German: [ˈhɛʁman ˈvɪlhɛlm ˈɡøːʁɪŋ] ; 12 January 1893 – 15 October 1946) was a German politician, aviator, military commander, and convicted war criminal.

description: municipality in Landkreis Hersfeld-Rotenburg in Hesse, Germany
description_wiki: Cornberg (German pronunciation: [ˈkɔʁnbɛʁk]) is a municipality in Hersfeld-Rotenburg district in northeastern Hesse, Germany.
Leipheim (German pronunciation: [ˈlaɪphaɪm] ; Swabian: Leiba) is a town in the district of Günzburg, in Bavaria, Germany.

description: seat of Werra-Meißner-Kreis and town in Hesse, Germany
description_wiki: Eschwege (German pronunciation: [ˈɛʃveːɡə] ), the district seat of the Werra-Meißner-Kreis, is a town in northeastern Hesse, Germany.
The region of Palestine, also known as historic Palestine or land of Palestine, is a geographical area in West Asia.

description: largest Christian Church, made up of 24 autonomous Churches in communion with the Pope, part of the Latin Church aka Latin Christianity aka western Christianity
description_wiki: The Catholic Church (Latin: Ecclesia Catholica), commonly called the Roman Catholic Church, is the largest Christian church, with an estimated 1.28 to 1.41 billion baptized members worldwide as of 2026.

aliases: The Holy Orthodox Catholic Apostolic Eastern Church
description_wiki: The Eastern Orthodox Church, officially the Orthodox Catholic Church, also known as the Greek Orthodox Church or simply the Orthodox Church, is a Christian communion of autocephalous national and regional Eastern Christian churches that adhere to Dyophysite Christology.
Baptists are a Protestant tradition of Christianity distinguished by baptizing only believers (believer's baptism) and doing so by total immersion.

surface_forms: United Nations Relief and Rehabilitation Administration
description_wiki: United Nations Relief and Rehabilitation Administration (UNRRA, pronounced  UN-rə) was an international relief agency founded in November 1943 on the joint initiative of the United States, United Kingdom, USSR, and the Republic of China.
Leipheim (German pronunciation: [ˈlaɪphaɪm] ; Swabian: Leiba) is a town in the district of Günzburg, in Bavaria, Germany.
Mike and Stefani is a 1952 Australian drama film produced by the Film Division, News and Information Bureau of the Department of Interior for the Department of Immigration.
Bad Aibling (German pronunciation: [baːt ˈʔaɪblɪŋ] ; Bavarian: Oabling) is a spa town and former district seat in Bavaria on the river Mangfall, located some 56 km (35 mi) southeast of Munich.
The American Friends Service Committee (AFSC) is a Quaker-founded organization working for peace and social justice in the United States and around the world.
Baltimore ( BAWL-tim-or, locally:  BAWL-dim-OR or  BAWL-mər), also known as Baltimore City, is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Maryland.
